pattern: Each equivalence pair unions two letters; a union-find keyed to the smallest letter in every group rewrites the base string to its minimal representative.
hints: The pairs build equivalence classes of letters; within a class any letter can stand for any other.
Use union-find over the 26 letters, but make the representative of a set its smallest letter so lookups give the minimal choice.
Union each s1[i] with s2[i] keeping the smaller letter as root, then map every character of baseStr to its root.

edge_inputs.description: small groups change only one letter

stress.description: many random pairs over the lowercase alphabet then a random base string
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1000)))
    s1 = ''.join(chr(97 + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(m))
    s2 = ''.join(chr(97 + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(m))
    bl = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    base = ''.join(chr(97 + rng.randint(0, 25)) for _ in range(bl))
    return [s1, s2, base]
stress.note: dense unions tend to collapse classes toward early letters
